title: Point Solutions Versus Integrated Oracle Applications: The Road to IT Investment ROI
author: Aberdeen Group (Katherine Jones, Tom Dwyer, Peter S. Kastner)
abstract: Oracle-sponsored Aberdeen Executive White Paper (June 2001) — SAME core research as study 6 ('Leaving Well Enough Alone', July 2001) but Oracle-branded. Conclusion substitutes 'Oracle provides such an application suite' for the vendor-neutral closing. Chronologically predates study 6 — this is likely the commissioned original; study 6 is the vendor-neutral generalization Aberdeen then released on its own behalf. Cites Beneficial Life and Indian Motorcycle (Oracle customers) only — drops Credit Suisse First Boston/Carreckers (PeopleSoft) and State of Michigan (Lawson) references that appear in study 6.
importance_rationale: Oracle-sponsored companion to the vendor-neutral Aberdeen ROI paper — historically significant as concrete evidence of the Aberdeen sponsored/sponsored-research model of the era, and of Oracle's use of analyst content in its sales materials.
relevance_rationale: ROI argument for integrated suites vs point solutions remains industry-foundational; pattern of sponsored-then-generalized analyst research remains relevant to AR-discipline study.
prescience_rationale: Predictions validated — Oracle did acquire PeopleSoft (2005) and Siebel (2006), consolidating the best-of-breed market as the paper implicitly anticipated.
